---
title: "6 Workflow: scripts and projects"
format: html
editor: visual
---

### **6.1 Scripts**

Open up the script editor by clicking the File menu, selecting New File, then R script, or using the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + Shift + N.

#### Running code

Ctrl + Enter executes the current R expression in the console.

It will run the complete command that the cursor is at and will also move the cursor to the following statement.

You can also execute the complete script in one step with Ctrl + Shift + S.

#### Saving and naming

RStudio automatically saves the contents of the script editor when you quit, and automatically reloads it when you re-open.

### **6.2 Projects**

#### What is the source of truth?

There is a great pair of keyboard shortcuts that will work together to make sure you’ve captured the important parts of your code in the editor:

Press Ctrl + Shift + F10 to restart R. Press Ctrl + Shift + S to re-run the current script.

#### Where does your analysis live?

You can print this out in R code by running getwd()

#### RStudio projects

```{r}

library(tidyverse)

ggplot(
  diamonds, 
  aes(x = carat, y = price)
) + 
  geom_hex()

ggsave("6_diamonds.png")

write_csv(diamonds, "diamonds.csv")

```
